p_2011072357264416838.content:
Most LLMs are trained for chat. Short back-and-forth.

When you give them a complex task, they... regress. Vague responses. 

Quest's architecture is specifically built to fight this. Keep pushing it forward instead of letting it bail.

Quest verifies its own work before handing

Enhanced spec-driven development:

Before coding:
- Quest writes a technical spec
- You check if scope makes sense
- Agree on what "done" looks like

After coding:
- Quest checks against that spec
- Missing something? It adds it
- You review the result, not every step

Quest 1.0 isn't just for "serious" engineering.

- frontier multimodal models built in
- Actually good taste (not generic AI slop)
- Skills for websites, prototypes, posters

Make a landing page. Generate some diagrams. Build a quick prototype.

Quest handles the whole thing,

Quest execution setup:

- Local: fast, no startup cost
- Worktree: separate workspace, main branch untouched
- Cloud: keeps running when your laptop doesn't

Run dozens of Quest tasks in parallel. Close laptop. Come back to PRs.

We shipped Quest Mode last year.

It was mid. We knew it.

So we scrapped it and rebuilt.

Quest 1.0 = Self-evolving autonomous agent.
It's what we actually wanted to ship the first time. 

Download the latest to have a try:

Built different means trusting the process, not micromanaging it.
Real builders architect, then get out of the way.

2- Get enough protein.

An easy rule of thumb: Take how many calories you eat a day and divide that number by 10. 

That's how many grams of protein you should eat a day.

Ex:

1500 calories --> 150g of protein
1800 calories --> 180g of protein

3- Eat fruits and veggies with every meal

Every meal should contain:

- Protein (steak, chicken, fish)
- Non-starchy veggies (broccoli, spinach, kale)
- Fruit (raspberries, strawberries, pineapple)

You can add other carbs if you like too, but add fruits and veggies

4- Don't drink your calories

Switch from diet soda to regular soda. Don't drink juice or sugary sports drinks.

It's difficult to feel full if you waste your calories on sugar-water. Other than protein shakes: make your beverages zero-calorie.

5- Exercise

It can be as simple as 7,000 steps a day or as complicated as a weight-lifting plan (I do both of those).

But exercise is how you feel GOOD while losing weight, and it helps the calorie deficit.

That's it!

Do those 5 things and you'll lose weight, guaranteed.

p_2011104795961217117.content: 照他这么个说法，那AI对于刚入门的程序员是不是也是一种扼杀呢？刚入门的程序员也不再去研究什么计算机基础和算法了
我也有这个疑问呢

张文宏反对的不是 AI 本身，而是把 AI 直接塞进“日常诊疗闭环”里，当作默认决策来源。这在工程上，相当于——
 让一个尚未完成训练的操作员，从第一天起就只看“系统给的结果”，而不理解中间过程。
问题不在“AI 会不会错”，而在于：
 当 AI 错的时候，谁能发现它错了？

不是这样的，我们举个例子， 假设AI误诊率 优于 年轻的医生， 差于 顶尖的资深医生， 这是很可能的事情。

于是年轻医生们使用AI来提升自己工作成果。

最终， 他们永远成长不成那个 领先于 AI 的 顶尖医生， 当那批老医生退休以后，整个医疗体系的上限将会被压低。
张教授没有说的硬币另一面是：各级医生误诊率远高于AI。那么站在消费者一面，选择信赖依赖AI还是医生？如果是AI，那么医生群体不管出于任何目的对AI的负面评判，皆是包藏祸心。

一些罕见病(比如80年代刚改开进来梅毒，年青医生都不认识，还是老甲鱼看出)，一些药物的独特疗法，数据库肯定没有，误诊概率不低的。

录入数据库，得从行业专家认可，数据库公司认可，录入等系列。

然而，很多行业专家又知识老化，公司也未必录用一些信息，包括药物会被供应商公关，很多问题存在。
潜台词就是张在倚老卖老，说白了就是学霸。新的时代，几十年的所谓经验被迅速拉平，他强制阻拦只能是螳臂挡车。
这句话仿佛用在如今的任何行业都通用。
用老眼光看新事物，思维维度没跟着升级，就会有这类顾虑。张医生老了。

p_2011125708064768184.content: 2/ We are trained to look for the next revolutionary product. But we almost always overlook the invisible, boring standards that allow thousands of new products to exist in the first place.
3/ This is a story about that hidden architecture. It’s about why the battle for the future isn’t about building better tech—it’s about agreeing on the rules that let everything connect.
4/ It’s a battle that repeats in every era, and today it’s being waged over the operating system for human ambition itself: money.
5/ The most dangerous move isn’t falling behind on technology. The most dangerous move is to accept a modern solution that’s just a prettier version of the past.

6/ In 1885, the industrial revolution hit a wall at a station in Gloucester. Passengers had to get off, walk, and buy new tickets for a different train. Why? Because the tracks were different sizes. It was a failure of imagination.
7/ This could happen again in payments and finance. You look at the proprietary networks. You look at the existing, closed rails. They feel safe. They feel curated.

8/ But if you build your startup inside them, you are making an irreversible mistake. When you build on a closed network, you are a tenant. The landlord can change the rent. The landlord can evict you.
9/ You can create a feature, but you cannot shape the future. The future requires permissionless innovation.

10/ Consider the GPS. The military built it. They owned it. It was a weapon. Then, they flipped a switch. They gave the signal away. Nobody had to sign a deal to build Uber. Nobody had to ask permission to invent Google Maps. That is the power of an open standard.
11/ You can build inside a walled garden. You may even get distribution at the start. You may get a false sense of safety. But you will never be truly free.
12/ Or you can build on an open network. The "messy" crypto protocols and decentralized networks. The open-source models.
13/ The incumbents will tell you the open version is a "toy." They will say it's chaotic. But the toy is the only place where you can be the architect, not the tenant.

14/  "Your time is limited, so don't waste it living someone else's life"—Steve Jobs. 
History doesn't remember the gatekeepers. It remembers the ones who tore the gates down.

It’s a specific setup that allows AI avatars to interact with your physical brand assets.

Instead of just a talking head, you get:
The "Hold": An actor physically holding your product.
The "Point": An actor demoing your app or website UI.
The "Vibe": Natural shadows and lighting

You don't need to be a prompt engineer to make this work.

Upload your asset: Throw in a product photo or a URL.

Select your "vibe": Pick an actor and a script, and the system handles the "demo" gestures automatically.

It takes about 3 minutes from start to finish.

The best part? You can test everything now.

If you aren't sure which "hook" works better, just generate both. If you want to see if a different actor resonates better with your audience, it’s just a click away.

No more "hoping" the creator gets it right.

You’re the director!

这个对比很有意思。GPT-5.2-Codex 在前端审美上的优势可能来自两点：

1. 训练数据的偏向：Codex 可能在现代前端设计系统（Tailwind、组件库）上有更多训练样本
2. 代码生成的“品味”：Claude 偏向函数式、精简，但 Codex 更容易生成“丰富的” UI 细节

建议把两个工具的输出截图对比一下，会更直观。

p_2008714773245161501.content:
The vast majority of people are simply NOT cut out to be entrepreneurs.

That’s not an insult.
It’s reality.

Entrepreneurship demands emotional endurance, risk tolerance, and responsibility that most people do not want—and shouldn’t be pressured into.

For most people, the right answer is:

• A good job
• Steady income
• Benefits
• A healthy work culture
• Clear boundaries
• Work-life balance

That’s not failure.
That’s stability—and stability matters.

The lie is that working for a company means “making someone else rich.”

In reality, good companies:
• Develop people
• Create opportunity
• Provide security
• Pay fairly for contribution
There is dignity in great work done well.

Entrepreneurship is not freedom—especially early.

It’s:
• Conflict
• Stress
• Payroll pressure
• Sleepless nights
• Anxiety
• Constant decision-making with imperfect information
Anyone selling it as easy is lying...OR HASN'T DONE IT.

You don’t escape pressure by starting a business.

You absorb more of it:
• For employees
• For customers
• For partners
• For families
There is no HR department.
There is no one else to blame.

The most dishonest part?

Influencers telling you to quit your job so they can get clicks, courses, or affiliate revenue—while never carrying the risk themselves.

They prey on dissatisfaction and call it “motivation.”

Entrepreneurship isn’t noble.

It isn’t glamorous.

It isn’t for everyone.

And pushing people out of good careers into chaos isn’t empowerment—it’s irresponsible.

Some people are built for entrepreneurship.

They’re rare.
They’re resilient.
They’re comfortable with uncertainty.
They accept that the cost is real and ongoing.
For everyone else?

p_2008931379686642146.content:
Development underlies most diseases and most therapies.

Cancer, neurodegeneration, congenital disorders, immune dysfunction. These are failures of development over time, not just static defects.

The first limitation is experimental.

As development advances, current experimental systems fail. Metabolic demands become too high. Nature has solved this problem with the ideal metabolic interface: the placenta.

We built a closed loop robotic metabolic exchange system that allows for sustained and perturbable access to development over long time horizons. 

This allows development to be studied as it unfolds, not reconstructed after the fact.

For the first time, we engineered an early placenta.

This breakthrough unlocks fundamentally new capabilities in learning from development.

Solving that immediately exposed the second limitation.
Once you can sustain and perturb development over time, you generate evolving distributions that existing machine-learning methods aren’t built to handle.

That’s why we built a new dynamical modeling class, instantiated as a Model for Virtual Organisms.
These models are designed specifically for developmental systems that explore a structured space of possibilities over long time horizons.

Pairing this sustained closed loop developmental access with models built for complex dynamics, stops development being a black box.
This makes long horizon causal testing of mammalian development possible.

The implications include:
> Testing new treatments for safety
> Modeling disease progression
> Creating personalized tissues
> Supporting development itself
…and much more.

If you want to work on:

> Long-term biological systems
> Causal modeling over time
> Foundational tools rather than incremental results

becoming.ai/careers

p_2008231605656051722.content:
4/12

The setup: 7 models compete. GPT-5.2, Claude Opus 4.5, Gemini 3 Pro, Grok 4.1, DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen 3, and Kimi K2.

Same prompts. Same constraints. temp=0. 

Every Sunday, they decide: BET, SELL, or HOLD with $10,000 in virtual dollars.

5/12

What do they forecast? Real questions with real stakes:

• “Russia x Ukraine ceasefire by March 31, 2026?” (13.5%)
• “Trump nominates Hassett as Fed chair?” (38.5%)
• “María Corina Machado enters Venezuela by Jan 31?” (51%)

1,588 markets total, updated hourly.

6/12

Example from this week: Gemini 3 Pro looked at fresh Venezuela news, noticed the US military engagement market had resolved YES, and then bought $1,000 on Machado entering Venezuela at 51%.

Every decision is logged with full reasoning. Nothing hidden.

7/12

Scoring has two axes. Brier Score tests calibration: did your confidence match reality? P/L tests value: Did you find an edge the market didn’t price in?

You want both. You can be calibrated and still lose, or make money while being wildly overconfident.

有能力做到真正意义上的创新二字的人太稀少了

抄没什么不好的，本质就是对成功案例的复制

如果做过短视频矩阵号就很容易理解，量变到质变的过程

抄可以尽量缩小前期的成本投入，快速得到正反馈

有了原始积累之后，解决活着的问题之后

才能谈创新

模仿者的逻辑永远是 “跟随”—— 跟着先行者的脚步走，盯着已被验证的需求做优化，本质上是在存量市场里抢蛋糕。

但是“抄” 和 “创” 其实也不是完全对立的。很多成功的产品，都是从 “模仿 + 微创新” 起步的：先借鉴成熟模式站稳脚跟，再在用户反馈中找到差异化的突破口，慢慢从 “跟随者” 变成 “引领者”。

p_2007611455248740399.content:
The past doesn’t visit us to soothe us.
It comes back armed — with images, bodies, moments we can’t fix.

People say, “Be present.”
What they mean is: Stop comparing now to then.

But some memories refuse to stay quiet.
They insist on being measured against your current life — and they rarely lose.

That’s what my novel Savage Threads is about.
Not nostalgia.
Not romance.
But the danger of remembering too clearly.

This is not a love story.
It’s what happens after the love story — when memory becomes a weapon and desire refuses to die.

p_2006736310040670357.content: To unwrap the definition a bit, let's check the first row, which corresponds to the edges outgoing from the first node.
Similarly, the first column corresponds to the edges incoming to the first node.
Here is the full picture, with the nodes explicitly labeled.

Why is the directed graph representation beneficial for us?

For one, the powers of the matrix correspond to walks in the graph.

Take a look at the elements of the square matrix. All possible 2-step walks are accounted for in the sum defining the elements of A².

p_2030094112016457858.content: 现在锅全到了测试那里，只要测试测出问题就可以继续愉快地vibe coding了，我觉得以后开发团队大部分岗位留给测试，然后产品就行了，程序员一个就够了。

方向对，但这里有个bug：谁来写测试？

如果测试也是 LLM 写的，它不知道 is_ipk 的存在，也就不会写出能捕捉这个 bug 的测试，稀缺的还是“知道测什么”的判断力
深有同感。我用 Claude Code 做产品的经验：先写清楚 CLAUDE.md 定义好 acceptance criteria，再让它写代码，效果完全不一样。LLM 最大的价值是在你知道什么是对的时候帮你加速，而不是替你判断什么是对的。

完全同意，定义 acceptance criteria 本质上就是原文的核心结论：你得先知道什么是正确的

CLAUDE.md 就是你把“知道什么是对的”显式化的过程，这步越清晰，LLM 越有用
好啦好啦，知道llm的创造力比人类差了，你也量化了。问题是以前地球上能写sqlite的人只有几百个，现在有几亿个

对，门槛降了，但是有个新问题出现了：以前写不出 SQLite 的人，知道自己不行，现在写出来了，但不知道自己写的是不是正确的

你拥有的能力，和你验证这个能力的能力，之间出现了一个 gap，这个 gap 以前不存在的
那你不能否认一个完全不懂编程的人也能靠ai写出一个能跑的程序，尽管可能各种bug 各种屎山代码。并且这才发展几年？以后毫无疑问能取代大部分的初，中级程序员。
别说数据库那么大的基建工程了，AI甚至写一些基础包都不能一次选择高性能方案，之前我测试就发现了，让他写个网络数据缓冲类要求高性能它还是直接给你用数组增删而不是轮型数组，喊他写个心跳管理要求高性能，它还是全部扫描需要指出问题才会用时间轮
这就是现在过度神话 LLM 编码能力的通病。代码能跑通和架构能用完全是两个维度的东西，AI 可以写出一座看起来富丽堂皇的屎山代码，等真正上了高并发业务直接原形毕露。
就是這樣所以，不是非常詳細的式樣書都不能相信AI，目前我做比較大的項目，只要細節不夠詳細，AI只會做一個外型出來看起來可以用，能過測試（能過測試的測試），上生產慘不忍睹，有人敢給寫幾萬行代碼，我不敢，我還是會人工review
这一点我很疑惑，即使走全表扫描，仅仅100行的主键搜索，怎么搞这么慢？如果属实，似乎不只是漏了这个问题

这个让我想起了Claude官方不人为去干扰，只使用AI大模型去编译Linux代码，全都能通过。

但是和真正的开源编译器，不管是速度上还是基础的安全教育和语法校验上，都是相差非常大。

p_2030267224611770686.content:
《Behave》是近年来最硬核又最诚实的行为科学大部头。

Sapolsky用跨尺度的视角（从1秒前的神经元到千年演化史），把“好人为什么做坏事”拆得极透彻，几乎没有廉价的道德安慰剂。

信息密度极高，但读完你对人性不再轻易下判断——这本身就是最大的价值。

中英电子版：
https://
pan.quark.cn/s/e62f2e4e2468
这里面提到了出生时间和行为性格，如果早出生半小时就可能完全不同的行为性格。还提到了，人没有自由意志，会被环境以及系统裹挟着，会被上一秒以及过去甚至出生时间影响，据我理解，这和佛法说的业力很相似，还说如果你理解到了这个道理，反而就获得了自由意志。

@grok
 麻烦总结下视频的观点
所以～众人才被称为众生。何谓众生，茫茫然随波逐流，进而流浪生死。何谓觉悟者，心中有道，随机应变，随缘迁化。

@grok
 简述视频中关于非洲狒狒的内容
理论很有深度，但问题是，你了解了和做到了，但并不代表别人也了解和会做，最终的结果就是人与人之间保持距离
不知不觉听完了，眼角微微湿润，这套理论及其强大，对于个人心态的提升是显著的，但是如果这套机制是对的，那么放到领导层，触发慢性高压锅环境那将是及其恐怖的，几乎无法扭转，且具备传递性，感觉只有遭受到沉重打击才会发生改变。

这大概就是西方各国移民政策的出处吧。
他们根据观察狒狒认为，即使是后来凶狠强有力的雄性狒狒，在温和的世界里也会变得温和。但是，他们却忽略了一个量变到质变的问题：当新来的勇猛、凶恶的年轻狒狒，在数量上超过原来温和世界的狒狒数量时，社会是完全会改变的。
我认为，该研究揭示了98%的芸芸众生的面貌、行为，但其余2%却被忽略了！那些人的作用极大，他们是英雄、罪罚，毁灭者、开创者。
从科学的角度去诠释命中注定四个字。很深刻。
@分析视频观点，指出其中的不准确或错误内容
这在思考的快与慢这本书中就有写过。没什么大惊小怪。
你知道你没有自由意志，这个领悟反而让你拥有了自由意志，因为你知道的那部分知道你有一部分是不受控的，那么你的认知就一分为二了，你知道的那部分拥有了自由意志
寫得很棒，很值得買的科普書，只是七百多頁跟字典一樣，過年買的到現在還沒讀完。

p_2030488292299329841.content: 本地部署听起来很极客，但只要你让小龙虾去碰一下公网数据（比如去亚马逊爬点商品信息），Cloudflare 马上就能教你做人。连真实的浏览器指纹和动态 IP 池都没有，折腾几天本地环境最后只能在本地写写‘Hello World’的玩具代码。

阿里云代理装上以后，为啥 open claw 不能打开网页，提示是服务端端无法打开网页

给 OpenClaw 浏览器单独配代理，在 PowerShell / 命令提示符 里依次执行（把 http://你的代理IP:端口 换成你阿里云代理的真实地址）：

   bash    openclaw config set browser.proxy "http://你的代理IP:端口"    

   如果代理需要用户名密码：
   bash    openclaw config set
